name: How much does door installation usually cost, including labor and materials?
headline: How much does door installation usually cost, including labor and materials?

mainEntity.name: How much does door installation usually cost, including labor and materials?
mainEntity.text: How much does door installation usually cost, including labor and materials?

acceptedAnswer.text: A new door might cost less than you think, but the total price includes both the door and the installation. For a basic interior prehung door, people commonly pay anywhere from a couple hundred dollars for the door plus similar or slightly more for labor. Exterior doors usually cost more because they are heavier, more secure, and need careful weatherproofing. Factors that drive cost include: door material (solid wood and fiberglass cost more than hollow-core), added glass or sidelights, smart locks or special hardware, and whether the opening needs new framing or repair. In coastal cities like Pacifica and Half Moon Bay, you may also pay a bit more for weather-rated materials and skilled labor familiar with stucco and moisture issues. Hidden costs can come from fixing rot, repairing stucco or siding, upgrading the threshold, or bringing the opening up to current code. When comparing quotes, ask if disposal of the old door, trim work, painting or staining, and hardware installation are included, or if they’re extra. A common pitfall is shopping only by lowest price. Very cheap installs sometimes skip important steps like proper flashing, insulation, or long screws into the framing. That can lead to drafts, leaks, and security problems. Aim for clear, itemized estimates, and make sure you understand what’s included so you don’t get surprised later.
